scope.summary:
Goal: Implement a diff and merge editor panel supporting side-by-side and inline (unified) diff views for both prose and code content. This provides parity with the KERNEL-009 MT-247 diff surface and ports the `document_diff_merge.ts` logic from the React codebase into a native egui panel. Also handles CRDT conflict UI: when a CRDT merge conflict exists in the workspace (signalled via a workspace state update), the merge editor opens with local/base/remote three-way view and per-block accept/reject controls. Expected diff shape: new `src/frontend/code_editor/diff_engine.rs`, new `src/frontend/panels/diff_editor_panel.rs`, edits to `src/frontend/code_editor/mod.rs`.

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/code_editor/diff_engine.rs` — ports `document_diff_merge.ts`. Defines `DiffBlock { left_lines: Range<usize>, right_lines: Range<usize>, status: DiffStatus }` where `DiffStatus` is an enum: `Equal`, `Added`, `Removed`, `Modified`. `DiffEngine::diff(left: &TextBuffer, right: &TextBuffer) -> Vec<DiffBlock>` — uses the Myers diff algorithm (implement from scratch or use the `similar` crate which is MIT-licensed and production-grade). For prose mode: works at the paragraph/block level (port `buildRichDocumentDiff` behavior for line-level comparison). `MergeBlock { base_lines: Range<usize>, local_lines: Range<usize>, remote_lines: Range<usize>, status: MergeStatus, chosen: Option<MergeChoice> }` where `MergeStatus: LocalOnly | RemoteOnly | BothSame | Conflict` and `MergeChoice: Local | Remote | Both`. `MergeEngine::three_way(base: &TextBuffer, local: &TextBuffer, remote: &TextBuffer) -> Vec<MergeBlock>` — computes local vs base diff and remote vs base diff, then aligns them to produce merge blocks.
- CREATE `src/frontend/panels/diff_editor_panel.rs` — `DiffEditorPanel` egui widget. State: `left: TextBuffer`, `right: TextBuffer`, `diff: Vec<DiffBlock>`, `mode: DiffMode` (enum: `SideBySide`, `Inline`). `DiffEditorPanel::show(ui)` renders: (a) SideBySide: two `CodeEditorPanel` instances side by side, synchronized scroll (when left scrolls, right scrolls to the same line, accounting for added/removed blocks), diff block backgrounds highlighted (green=added, red=removed, yellow=modified). (b) Inline: single panel showing both left and right lines interleaved with change markers. Toggle button for SideBySide vs Inline in the panel header. Also supports `MergeMode`: three panes (local, base, remote) with per-block accept buttons.
- EDIT `src/frontend/code_editor/mod.rs` — add `open_diff(left: TextBuffer, right: TextBuffer)` and `open_merge(base, local, remote: TextBuffer)` factory methods that construct a `DiffEditorPanel` and place it in the egui tile layout as a new tile.

Exact crates/functions/APIs to use:
- `similar = "2"` (MIT) — production-grade diff library used by many Rust projects; provides Myers diff over slices. Use `similar::ChangeTag` and `similar::TextDiff::from_lines` for line-level diff.
- `egui::SidePanel` or manual `ui.columns(2, ...)` for the side-by-side layout.
- `egui::Painter::rect_filled` — for diff block background highlights.
- `egui::Button` — for merge choice accept buttons (Accept Local, Accept Remote, Accept Both).
- AccessKit: `author_id = "diff_editor_panel"` `Role::Group`; `author_id = "diff_block_{n}_accept_local"` `Role::Button` on each merge accept button; `author_id = "diff_mode_toggle"` `Role::ToggleButton` on the SideBySide/Inline toggle.

React reference files:
- `app/src/lib/editor/document_diff_merge.ts` — complete reference for diff and merge data structures. Port: `RichDocumentDiff`, `RichDocumentDiffBlock`, `RichDocumentDiffStatus`, `RichDocumentMergePlan`, `RichDocumentMergeBlock`, `RichDocumentMergeStatus`, `buildRichDocumentDiff`, `buildRichDocumentMergePlan`, `applyRichDocumentMergeChoices`. Native equivalents use `TextBuffer` instead of `JSONContent` (the prose format). For code buffers, the diff is line-level not block-level.
- `app/src/lib/editor/document_diff_merge.test.ts` — test cases for the diff/merge logic; port these tests as Rust unit tests.
- `app/src/harness/rich_document_diff_harness.tsx` — the visual harness for the React diff editor; the native egui panel must match its feature surface.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Use `similar::TextDiff::from_lines(left_text, right_text)` for line-level diff. Map `ChangeTag::Equal` -> `DiffStatus::Equal`, `Insert` -> `Added`, `Delete` -> `Removed`, and consecutive Delete+Insert at the same position -> `Modified`.
2. Side-by-side sync scroll: maintain a `sync_scroll: SyncScrollState { left_scroll_offset: f32, right_scroll_offset: f32, last_changed: Side }`. When the left ScrollArea changes scroll offset, compute the corresponding right offset by counting visible lines in diff blocks and mapping them. Use `VirtualLineLayout` from MT-002 for each pane.
3. Diff block backgrounds: before rendering each line in the virtual line loop, check if the line falls in a `DiffBlock` and set `ui.painter().rect_filled(line_rect, 0, diff_color)` where `diff_color` is: Added=Color32::from_rgba_unmultiplied(0,255,0,30), Removed=Color32::from_rgba_unmultiplied(255,0,0,30), Modified=Color32::from_rgba_unmultiplied(255,200,0,30).
4. Inline mode: render left lines normally; for Added blocks, add a green `+ ` prefix; for Removed, a red `- ` prefix; for Equal, a gray `  ` prefix. No side-by-side layout needed.
5. Merge mode per-block UI: for each `MergeBlock` with `status=Conflict`, render three rows (local, base, remote) with Accept Local / Accept Remote / Accept Both buttons. On accept, set `block.chosen` and recompute the merged output buffer.
6. Synchronized scroll algorithm: maintain a `line_map: Vec<(Option<usize>, Option<usize>)>` where each entry is (left_line, right_line) for the corresponding visual row (None for lines that only exist on one side). When left scrolls to visual row R, find `line_map[R].1` and scroll right to that row.

Dependencies/unblock conditions: MT-001 (TextBuffer for both panes), MT-002 (VirtualLineLayout for both panes). Does NOT require MT-005, MT-007, or MT-008 — the diff editor is a standalone panel.
Risk if missed: No diff view means code review workflows are blocked. CRDT conflict resolution has no UI. This is parity with KERNEL-009 MT-247 which is already validated.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend diff_engine` passes — port the test cases from `document_diff_merge.test.ts`: identical texts -> all Equal blocks; added line -> one Added block; removed line -> one Removed block; modified line -> one Modified block.

AC-002: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend merge_engine` passes — tests: local adds line 2, remote adds line 5 to same base -> two LocalOnly/RemoteOnly blocks (no conflict); both modify line 3 differently -> one Conflict block.
AC-003: egui_kittest screenshot in SideBySide mode shows two panes with a green background rect in the right pane for an added line.
AC-004: egui_kittest screenshot in Inline mode shows `+ ` prefix (green colored) on an added line and `- ` prefix (red colored) on a removed line.
AC-005: egui_kittest interactive test: merge mode with one Conflict block; click 'Accept Local' button; verify the merged output buffer contains the local version of the conflicted block.
AC-006: AccessKit tree in merge mode contains at least one button node with author_id matching 'diff_block_0_accept_local'.
AC-007: Synchronized scroll test: scroll left pane by 20 lines; verify right pane scrolled to the equivalent position (within ±2 lines).
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend diff_engine` — GREEN, logged.
PT-002: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend merge_engine` — GREEN, logged.
PT-003: egui_kittest screenshot `test_output/MT-009-sidebyside-diff.png` pixel-verified for green background in right pane.
PT-004: egui_kittest screenshot `test_output/MT-009-inline-diff.png` pixel-verified for colored prefixes.
PT-005: egui_kittest test `merge_accept_local` — merged buffer content verified.
PT-006: egui_kittest test `diff_editor_accesskit` — AccessKit dump confirms accept button node.
implementation_notes: `similar` crate: prefer `TextDiff::from_lines` which returns an iterator of `Change` items. Collect into `DiffBlock` ranges by grouping consecutive equal tags.

For very large files, the diff computation may be slow — run it on a background thread via `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` and update `diff_blocks` asynchronously. Show a 'computing diff...' indicator while pending.
CRDT conflict signal: listen for a workspace state update event (to be defined in CRDT integration). For now, accept `open_merge(base, local, remote)` as a direct API call from the orchestrator.

Prose mode: for JSON content diffs (rich documents), the diff is block-level not line-level. Port `buildRichDocumentDiff` as an alternative entry point that takes `serde_json::Value` pairs and produces `DiffBlock`s at the JSON node level.
ports_from_react: app/src/lib/editor/document_diff_merge.ts — complete file: RichDocumentDiff, RichDocumentMergePlan, buildRichDocumentDiff, buildRichDocumentMergePlan, applyRichDocumentMergeChoices — port all as Rust equivalents
app/src/lib/editor/document_diff_merge.test.ts — port test cases as Rust unit tests in diff_engine.rs
app/src/harness/rich_document_diff_harness.tsx — visual harness reference for feature parity checklist
binds_backend_api: None for diff computation — purely in-process. CRDT conflict data arrives via workspace state events (separate integration). No HTTP calls in this MT.

risks: RISK-001: `similar` diff on a 100k-line file takes >1 second on the main thread, blocking the egui frame. Mitigate: spawn on `tokio::task::spawn_blocking`; show spinner; do not re-trigger diff computation on every frame — only on buffer version change.

RISK-002: Synchronized scroll mapping is wrong for files with many added/removed blocks — the visual row count differs from the line count. Mitigate: unit test the line_map construction with known added/removed blocks and verify the mapping is correct.

RISK-003: Merge mode accept-all without a conflict generates an incorrect merged buffer if the merge algorithm has a bug in three-way merge. Mitigate: port the exact test cases from `document_diff_merge.test.ts` before shipping.

RISK-004: Two CodeEditorPanel instances in side-by-side mode use the same egui Id for the ScrollArea, causing scroll state collision. Mitigate: suffix the egui Id with 'left' and 'right' respectively.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Background diff computation is required for files > 10k lines — documented and implemented.
MC-002: Ported test cases from document_diff_merge.test.ts are a required AC.
MC-003: SideBySide egui Id suffix is required — verified by screenshot AC-003 (two distinct scroll positions).
